title: UNRWA head Philippe Lazzarini's grim assessment: 'Without firm political and financial support, we face a slow death'
subhead: The commissioner-general of the United Nations agency for Palestinian refugees, whose term ends on March 31, voices deep concern for the survival of the underfunded organization that has destabilized by Israeli attacks and disregard for international law.

title: France's aircraft carrier located in real time by Le Monde through Strava data
Trump says considering 'winding down' war as US eases Iranian oil sanctions
US jury finds Elon Musk misled Twitter shareholders
subhead: According to damages calculated by jurors, Musk could be ordered to pay out as much as $2.6 billion. Musk abandoned his effort to get out of buying Twitter in late 2022 after the company took him to court to uphold the contract. The tech billionaire has since merged the social media platform – now called X – with SpaceX and his startup xAI.

title: 'With Operation Epic Fury, the US turns pop culture into the language of Pentagon violence'
subhead: In this op-ed for Le Monde, Louis Lapeyrie, a researcher at Paris-I Panthéon-Sorbonne University, examines the visual and narrative shifts in military communications under the Trump administration and explores the many borrowings from video games.

title: An ultra-modern artist: 'Renoir and Love' and 'Renoir Drawings' at the Musée d'Orsay
subhead: Challenging his reputation as a sentimental artist, two exhibitions of paintings and drawings highlight Renoir's affinity for working-class subjects and the complexity of his compositions.

title: 'I am ashamed to have all this unearned money': As the baby boomer generation passes on its wealth, young heirs grapple with guilt and discomfort
subhead: Receiving a large sum of money at a young age is anything but trivial. Early inheritances and gifts often bring discomfort, guilt and silence for those who benefit, and deepen inequalities.

title: White House says it could 'take out' Iran's Kharg island anytime

Anne-Claire Legendre, new president of France's Arab World Institute: 'Our role is to provide tools for understanding'
Catherine MacGregor, Engie CEO, on gas prices: 'There are many differences compared to 2022'
Anger erupts in Sicily after US Navy helicopters land in a protected area
Putin conducts a 'special musical operation' in Europe
In Paris, Reza Pahlavi, son of Iran's last shah, builds his network
subhead: Reza Pahlavi has been based in the French capital since the US-Israeli strikes on Iran started, making moves in anticipation of the Islamic Republic's fall.

title: Putin conducts a 'special musical operation' in Europe
subhead: Barred from the West, Valery Gergiev, maestro at the helm of Russia's two leading opera houses and cultural ambassador for Vladimir Putin, is seeking a return to conducting in Europe's major concert halls. In this cultural arena, Moscow is shifting from 'soft power' to 'sharp power.'

title: Bertrand Badie, political scientist: 'Persian culture is an impetuous alliance of dignity and fury'
subhead: The international relations specialist, who is of Iranian descent on his father's side, speaks with Le Monde about the West's lack of understanding of Iran and its culture.

title: War in Iran: 'Europe said no to the US. This is new, and it is a major development'
'The US neoconservative plan to reshape the Middle East did not bring democracy, but civil war'
Europeans' justified mistrust in Trump's war in Iran
